description: React (Like or other reaction) to a LinkedIn post by URL. Navigates to the post, checks if already reacted (idempotent guard), and clicks the Like/React button if not already active. Verifies the reaction was registered.

reasoning.research: LinkedIn's Like button is a toggle. If already liked, clicking it un-likes. Idempotency is critical: check the button's aria-pressed state or active CSS class before clicking. The reaction button has role=button with name containing 'Like' and aria-pressed='true' when active.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use role=button[name='Like'] as primary selector. The button's aria-pressed attribute indicates current state ('true' = already liked). For other reactions (Celebrate, Support, Love, etc.), the reaction picker appears on hover/long-press. MVP only implements 'like' reaction.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: No fill_slowly needed — just a click. Wait 1s after click for LinkedIn API to register and update the UI count. Check aria-pressed before and after to verify state change.
reasoning.llm_learnings: LinkedIn reaction button states: aria-pressed='false' (not reacted) → click → aria-pressed='true' (reacted). The count updates asynchronously. For idempotency: if aria-pressed is already 'true', skip the click and return reacted=true. Button is inside .reactions-react-button container.
reasoning.performance: Navigation 3s + state check 0.2s + optional click 0.5s + verify 0.3s. Total: ~4-5s.

post_url.description: Full URL of the LinkedIn post to react to.

reaction.description: Reaction type. Currently only 'like' is supported in MVP.

post_page.note: Navigate to the post to see the Like button.

like_button.note: Like button. aria-pressed=true means already liked — skip click (idempotent).

like_button_fallback.note: Class-based fallback for the reaction container.

reaction_count.note: Post-click verification: read the updated reaction count.

execution_trace.note: Navigate to post. Must be logged in.
Check if already liked. If 'true', skip steps 3-4 (idempotent guard).
Click Like only if not already reacted.

was_already_reacted.description: True if the post was already liked before this run (idempotent case).

metadata.safety_note: Idempotent: if post is already liked, returns reacted=true without clicking. Does not un-like.
